items.title: This Qi2 15W 5K MagSafe power bank is the thinnest one in my EDC, and you can snag it for just $17 today, more from $5.50
items.summary:
We’re officially in Day 3 of Amazon’s Big Spring Sale, and alongside the big-ticket tech deals, we’re also seeing some seriously solid savings on everyday accessories. LISEN’s official Amazon storefront is now offering notable discounts across its lineup of charging gear, car mounts, cables, organizers, and tablet accessories. Whether you’re upgrading your in-car setup, tidying up your desk, or simply stocking up on extra cables, there are plenty of practical options to consider. We’re seeing deals of up to 60% off, with prices starting from just $5.50 Prime shipped, making this a great time to grab useful essentials without stretching the budget.

One standout offer gets you the brand’s 15W Qi2-certified 5,000mAh MagSafe power bank for $16.99 Prime shipped when you apply promo code DN5EPS2U at checkout. This model typically sells for $30, so you’re looking at nearly 50% off. It’s also one of the thinnest power banks around, making it easy to carry daily, and the newer Qi2 support helps deliver faster charging compared to the standard version.

more…

items.title: Bluetti’s increased spring savings are now taking up to 58% off power stations starting from $329 + exclusive bonus savings
items.summary:
Running parallel to Amazon’s Big Spring Sale, Bluetti has launched the final phase of its own direct Spring Sale with increased discounts of up to 58% that is only running through the rest of March – plus, we have some exclusive bonus savings codes to give our readers even lower prices! One notable increase in savings from the previous phase is hitting Bluetti’s popular 2,764.8Wh Apex 300 Versatile Portable Power Station at $1,371.80 shipped, after using the code 9TO5TOYS5OFF at checkout, beating out its Amazon storefront pricing by $72. It may carry a $2,199 MSRP, but you can more often find it down around $1,699 these days. Discounts since its May 2025 release have mostly kept costs above $1,399, though there was a one-time $1,299 low that appeared during Black Friday. During the short window left, you can score the next-best price, courtesy of the $327 markdown off the going rate (and $827 off the MSRP). Head below to learn more, get our exclusive codes, and browse the full lineup of deals while they last through March 31, 2026.

items.title: Today only: ASUS’ Vivobook 14 Snapdragon Copilot+ PC is down to $400 (46% off)
items.summary:
As part of its Deals of the Day offers, Best Buy is offering ASUS’ Vivobook 14 Snapdragon X-powered Copilot+ notebook in Quiet Blue finish for $399.99 shipped. This is originally a $700 laptop that has lately been fetching $750 at Best Buy, Amazon, and others, meaning you’re looking at a 46% discount on it right now. Today’s deal shaves $350 off its usual going rate, making this a great time to cash in. While the AMD-powered version of this 14-inch Vivobook has seen frequent discounts in the past, the Snapdragon X model featured here isn’t nearly as frequently marked down. It’s now fetching $20 more than its previous all-time low and our mention from last month when it hit the $380 mark as part of a similar daily deals offer, but still lands among the best prices we have tracked to date.

items.summary:
As part of the Amazon Big Spring Sale, the official Anker storefront is offering the best price yet on its new Soundcore Nebula X1 Pro Triple Laser 4K Home & Outdoor Cinema Projector for $3,999 shipped. Since its reveal and release at CES 2026, this all-new projector has mostly stayed at its $4,999 MSRP, though we have seen one previous discount to $4,299 that occurred during the first half of February. If you held off on buying it then, you’re getting an even larger 20% markdown here for the rest of March, cutting $1,000 off the cost for a new all-time low price. What’s more, there’s even a bundle option for the projector and a 200-inch inflatable screen, giving you a mobile theater unlike any other at $4,999 shipped, down from $6,998. For cheaper alternatives for your mobile streamings/movie nights, be sure to check out the Anker Nebula roundup of projectors and accessories with up to 35% discounts starting from $32.

items.title: Gotrax’s 55-mile range R2 folding e-bike at $649 ($251 off) or the 18-mile XR Elite Max e-scooter at a $320 low, more from $100
items.summary:
As part of the ongoing Amazon Big Spring Sale, you can currently find Gotrax’s various e-bikes and e-scooters with up to 28% discounts hitting them starting from $100. Among the many models we’re seeing in this sale, a top pick for folks on a commuting budget is Gotrax’s R2 Folding e-bike down at $649 shipped from a $900 full price tag. Over the last year, discounts have mostly kept prices above $656, though there was a one-time fall to the $638 low for Cyber Monday. That being said, the deal here gives you the next-best price with a $251 markdown that lands it just $11 above that one-time low. It’s a handy means to travel up to 55 miles using the pedal assistance, maxing out at 20 MPH speeds. Of course, if you want a cheaper option for smaller commutes around neighborhoods, college campuses, and the like, be sure to check out the 18-mile traveling XR ELITE MAX Electric Scooter down at its $320 low from $420. You’ll find many more models rounded up below for your one-stop shopping needs.

Over at Lenovo, you’ll find the brand’s Idea Tab Pro Android tablet currently going for $237.49 shipped. Don’t forget to enter promo code EXTRAFIVE during checkout to see the discounted price. This particular tablet, which comes bundled with the Lenovo Tab Pen Plus stylus, typically sells for $390, so you’re looking at a straight-up $152 discount right now. It’s only been discounted once in the past, and today’s offer beats that by $10 to mark a new best price. Best Buy currently lists this base variant for $310 with a lighter discount, but Lenovo’s offer with the above-mentioned promo code knocks a significant chunk off the regular price to land at a new all-time low.

items.title: Netflix Hikes Prices Once Again; Malaysia Not Yet Affected
